scenario: Nimbus fintech is rebuilding a payments service on Spring Boot for Camunda 8.8 that consumes 12000 jobs per day. The team must pick the official integration library and align with the documented migration path away from the legacy SDK before any later upgrade.
question: Which integration library is the official Spring Boot path for Camunda 8.8?

options.text: The Spring Zeebe SDK remains the supported Spring Boot integration for Camunda 8.8 and is scheduled to keep that role across the entire 8.x release series
The Camunda Spring Boot Starter is the official Camunda 8.8 integration and replaces the Spring Zeebe SDK as of version 8.8 with REST as the default protocol
The Camunda Java Client is the official Spring Boot integration for Camunda 8.8 and developers add it directly without any Spring Boot starter dependency
The Camunda REST connector is the official Spring Boot integration for Camunda 8.8 and replaces the Spring Zeebe SDK as part of the 8.8 release line

a.text: Incorrect. The Spring Zeebe SDK is being phased out and will be removed in version 8.10; teams must migrate to the Camunda Spring Boot Starter before upgrading past 8.8.

b.text: Correct. The Camunda Spring Boot Starter is the official Camunda 8.8 integration; it replaces the Spring Zeebe SDK as of 8.8, uses the new Camunda Java Client under the hood, and defaults to REST with gRPC configurable.

c.text: Incorrect. The Camunda Java Client powers the starter under the hood but is not the Spring Boot integration itself; Spring Boot projects add the Camunda Spring Boot Starter.

d.text: Incorrect. There is no REST connector that replaces the SDK; the official replacement for the Spring Zeebe SDK as of 8.8 is the Camunda Spring Boot Starter.

explanation: As of Camunda 8.8 the official Spring Boot integration is the Camunda Spring Boot Starter, which replaces the Spring Zeebe SDK. It uses the Camunda Java Client under the hood and defaults to REST with gRPC available as a configurable protocol. The legacy Spring Zeebe SDK will be removed in version 8.10, so teams must migrate before then.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot Starter getting started
